hypothesis: Fix hyperparams to trigger phase transition on n=20/k=3
notes: LR=0.1 is critical. LR=0.5 overshoots, never triggers grokking.

hypothesis: GrokFast accelerates grokking when hyperparams already correct
notes: GrokFast counterproductive: 83x more weight movement, 12 vs 5 epochs. Amplifies gradients that don't need amplifying.

hypothesis: Per-layer forward-backward reduces ARD on winning config
notes: Converges identically. W1 dominance caps further gains at ~10%.

hypothesis: Batch=32 improves ARD via parameter reuse
notes: ARD metric can't capture batch benefit. Batch wins on total traffic (13% fewer floats), not locality.

hypothesis: Per-layer converges on 20-bit with winning config
notes: 99.5% accuracy, identical convergence speed. 3.8% ARD improvement confirmed at scale.

hypothesis: Find where SGD breaks on scaling
notes: n=30 solved (94.5%), n=50 failed (54%). Breaks at n^k > 100K steps. k=5 appeared impractical (later disproved).

hypothesis: Forward-Forward has lower ARD than backprop
notes: FF fails on 20-bit (58.5%). 25x worse ARD. Greedy layer-wise learning can't coordinate for parity.

hypothesis: Higher weight decay accelerates grokking
notes: WD=0.01 optimal. Only [0.01,0.05] works. WD>=0.1 too strong, WD<0.01 no grokking in 200 epochs.

hypothesis: Curriculum (n=10 then n=50) bypasses scaling wall
notes: 14.6x speedup. Transfer after W1 expansion is instant. Solves n=50/k=3 which SGD cannot.

hypothesis: Per-layer + batch=32 combine for ARD improvement
notes: Converges identically (40.6 vs 41.4 epochs). 3.7x wall-time overhead from re-forward pass. Single-sample is 8x faster in epochs.

hypothesis: Cache model shows batch wins on locality
notes: L2 (256KB) eliminates ALL misses for both methods. Single-sample is MORE L1-friendly (91-100% vs 69-73%). Batch wins on traffic (13% fewer floats), not locality.

hypothesis: Sign SGD solves k=5 faster than standard SGD
notes: 2x faster (7 vs 14 epochs to 90%). But standard SGD also works with n_train=5000. Real bottleneck was training data.

hypothesis: Random/evolutionary search over k-subsets
notes: Solves all configs including n=50/k=3 (which SGD fails). Random: 881-18K tries, <0.5s. Combinatorial search bypasses grokking.

hypothesis: Feature selection methods beat brute-force
notes: Exhaustive combo search 178-1203x ops speedup. But pairwise/greedy provably fail -- parity invisible below order k. E[y*x_i*x_j]=0 for ALL pairs.

hypothesis: Walsh-Hadamard Fourier correlation finds secret
notes: 13x faster than SGD. 100% on all configs. Only needs 20 samples for k=3. But ARD is 64x worse -- pure streaming, no locality.

hypothesis: Mutual information detects parity better than Fourier
notes: 3.7x slower, same ARD. True subset MI ~0.693 nats (log 2), wrong subsets ~0.001. No advantage over Fourier.

hypothesis: LASSO on interaction features finds parity
notes: Robust to alpha across 500x range. Finds exactly 1 nonzero coefficient. Same combinatorial cost as Fourier O(C(n,k)).

hypothesis: Decision trees learn parity via greedy splits
notes: Best is ExtraTrees at 92.5%. Greedy info-gain splitting fails because individual bits have zero marginal correlation.

hypothesis: GF(2) Gaussian elimination solves parity algebraically
notes: 240x faster than SGD. k-independent. Only needs n+1 samples. Solves n=100/k=10 in 703us.

hypothesis: Monte Carlo Fourier subsampling reduces eval count
notes: Saves 30-70% of evaluations vs exhaustive but high variance (geometric distribution). Modest wall-time speedup (1.1-1.5x).

hypothesis: KM influence estimation finds secret in O(n)
notes: 724x better ARD than Fourier. 5 influence samples per bit suffice. O(n) not O(C(n,k)).

hypothesis: Hebbian learning detects parity via local correlations
notes: Chance level. Parity requires k-th order interactions. Hebbian only sees 1st-order correlations which are exactly zero.

hypothesis: Predictive coding has lower ARD than backprop
notes: Failed (51-55%). 18x worse ARD. 15 inference iterations re-read weight matrices ~32 times. Generative model harder than discriminative for parity.

hypothesis: Equilibrium propagation solves parity with local updates
notes: Failed (~60%). 2300x slower than SGD. 60 relaxation iterations (30 free + 30 clamped). Tanh saturation kills it.

hypothesis: Target propagation uses local targets for layer-wise learning
notes: Failed (~55%). Linear inverse G2 produces input-independent targets (target collapse). ARD 1.1-1.6x worse than backprop.

hypothesis: Tiled W1 updates reduce ARD via cache locality
notes: Software ARD increased 6.8-12.1%. MemTracker can't capture hardware cache benefits. Output layer needs full hidden vector.

hypothesis: Pebble game finds optimal execution order for energy
notes: 2.2% energy win (10.76 vs 11.00 uJ). But fused/per-layer orderings break training (57%) via read-after-write hazard on mutable params.

hypothesis: Binary weights solve parity with fewer ops
notes: Solves n=3/k=3 in 1 epoch (80x faster) but fails n=20/k=3 (~55%). STE too crude for feature selection.

hypothesis: GP evolves exact symbolic solution
notes: Finds mul(x[0],mul(x[15],x[17])). Zero params, zero ARD. But fails n=50/k=3 and n=20/k=5 -- needle-in-haystack fitness landscape.

hypothesis: SMT/backtracking constraint solver finds secret
notes: k-1 pruning trick: once k-1 indices fixed, last column is fully determined. Only 10 samples needed.

hypothesis: RL learns to query only the relevant bits
notes: Reads exactly k=3 bits per prediction. ARD=1 at inference. Value-blind state was key to making Q-learning converge.

hypothesis: MDL finds secret via compression
notes: Works and is noise-robust (5% label noise ok). True subset compresses to 0 bits, wrong subsets ~499 bits. 30% slower than Fourier.

hypothesis: Germain's hidden=64 is a locality win
notes: ARD per float accessed is identical (0.367 vs 0.368). The improvement is the model being 68% smaller, not better locality.

hypothesis: GrokFast helps when grokking plateau is genuinely long (high k)
notes: WIN on k=5 (2.5x speedup), LOSS on n=30/k=3 (40% solve rate), NEUTRAL on n=20/k=3. Interaction order is the critical variable, not input dimension.
